{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|27|17.3|N|135|42|39|E}}}} |Location = Narahara 1662, Gose City, Nara Prefecture |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 17.3 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 39 |Enshrined deities = Kimata-no-Kami, and Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha, former village shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festivals = July 13, October 16 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Komagata-Ōshige Shrine''' (こまがたおおしげじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Narahara, Gose City, Nara Prefecture. === Overview === In 1907 (Meiji 40), Ōshige Shrine, which had been enshrined in the eastern area known as Taguchi, was merged into Komagata Shrine, forming its present configuration.[https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001414.html Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Gose City[https://kotobank.jp/word/駒形大重神社-3081535 Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Kotobank. While Komagata Shrine enshrines a local tutelary deity known in legend as Kimata-sama, Ōshige Shrine is identified with “Katsuraki Ōshige Shrine” listed in the Yamato Province, Katsukami District section of the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, making it a Shikinaisha. Its enshrined deity is said to be either Shigeno no Sadamochi or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida.. === Enshrined deities === * Kimata-no-Kami * Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida === Shrine grounds === Komagata-Ōshige Shrine auxiliary shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Auxiliary shrine Stone frog called “Buji Kaeru” at Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|“Buji Kaeru” (Safe-return frog) Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Second torii gate Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio (1).jpg|First torii gate === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:moved from a redlink]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * カツラキ- == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市朝町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:駒形大重神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134928901}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Komagata-Ōshige Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|27|17.3|N|135|42|39|E}}}} |Location = Narahara 1662, Gose City, Nara Prefecture |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 17.3 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 39 |Enshrined deities = Kimata-no-Kami, and Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha, former village shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festivals = July 13, October 16 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Komagata-Ōshige Shrine''' (こまがたおおしげじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Narahara, Gose City, Nara Prefecture. === Overview === In 1907 (Meiji 40), Ōshige Shrine, which had been enshrined in the eastern area known as Taguchi, was merged into Komagata Shrine, forming its present configuration.[https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001414.html Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Gose City[https://kotobank.jp/word/駒形大重神社-3081535 Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Kotobank. While Komagata Shrine enshrines a local tutelary deity known in legend as Kimata-sama, Ōshige Shrine is identified with “Katsuraki Ōshige Shrine” listed in the Yamato Province, Katsukami District section of the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, making it a Shikinaisha. Its enshrined deity is said to be either Shigeno no Sadamochi or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida.. === Enshrined deities === * Kimata-no-Kami * Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida === Shrine grounds === Komagata-Ōshige Shrine auxiliary shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Auxiliary shrine Stone frog called “Buji Kaeru” at Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|“Buji Kaeru” (Safe-return frog) Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Second torii gate Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio (1).jpg|First torii gate === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * カツラキ- == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市朝町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:駒形大重神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134928901}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Komagata-Ōshige Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|27|17.3|N|135|42|39|E}}}} |Location = Narahara 1662, Gose City, Nara Prefecture |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 17.3 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 39 |Enshrined deities = Kimata-no-Kami, and Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha, former village shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festivals = July 13, October 16 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Komagata-Ōshige Shrine''' (こまがたおおしげじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Narahara, Gose City, Nara Prefecture. === Overview === In 1907 (Meiji 40), Ōshige Shrine, which had been enshrined in the eastern area known as Taguchi, was merged into Komagata Shrine, forming its present configuration.[https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001414.html Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Gose City[https://kotobank.jp/word/駒形大重神社-3081535 Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Kotobank. While Komagata Shrine enshrines a local tutelary deity known in legend as Kimata-sama, Ōshige Shrine is identified with “Katsuraki Ōshige Shrine” listed in the Yamato Province, Katsukami District section of the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, making it a Shikinaisha. Its enshrined deity is said to be either Shigeno no Sadamochi or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida.. === Enshrined deities === * Kimata-no-Kami * Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida === Shrine grounds === Komagata-Ōshige Shrine auxiliary shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Auxiliary shrine Stone frog called “Buji Kaeru” at Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|“Buji Kaeru” (Safe-return frog) Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Second torii gate Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio (1).jpg|First torii gate === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * カツラキ- == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市朝町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:駒形大重神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134928901}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Komagata-Ōshige Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|27|17.3|N|135|42|39|E}}}} |Location = Narahara 1662, Gose City, Nara Prefecture |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 17.3 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 39 |Enshrined deities = Kimata-no-Kami, and Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha, former village shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festivals = July 13, October 16 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Komagata-Ōshige Shrine''' (こまがたおおしげじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Narahara, Gose City, Nara Prefecture. === Overview === In 1907 (Meiji 40), Ōshige Shrine, which had been enshrined in the eastern area known as Taguchi, was merged into Komagata Shrine, forming its present configuration.[https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001414.html Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Gose City[https://kotobank.jp/word/駒形大重神社-3081535 Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Kotobank. While Komagata Shrine enshrines a local tutelary deity known in legend as Kimata-sama, Ōshige Shrine is identified with “Katsuraki Ōshige Shrine” listed in the Yamato Province, Katsukami District section of the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, making it a Shikinaisha. Its enshrined deity is said to be either Shigeno no Sadamochi or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida.. === Enshrined deities === * Kimata-no-Kami * Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida === Shrine grounds === Komagata-Ōshige Shrine auxiliary shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Auxiliary shrine Stone frog called “Buji Kaeru” at Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|“Buji Kaeru” (Safe-return frog) Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Second torii gate Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio (1).jpg|First torii gate === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

Komagata-Ōshige Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|27|17.3|N|135|42|39|E}}}} |Location = Narahara 1662, Gose City, Nara Prefecture |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 17.3 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 39 |Enshrined deities = Kimata-no-Kami, and Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha, former village shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festivals = July 13, October 16 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Komagata-Ōshige Shrine''' (こまがたおおしげじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Narahara, Gose City, Nara Prefecture. === Overview === In 1907 (Meiji 40), Ōshige Shrine, which had been enshrined in the eastern area known as Taguchi, was merged into Komagata Shrine, forming its present configuration.[https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001414.html Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Gose City[https://kotobank.jp/word/駒形大重神社-3081535 Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Kotobank. While Komagata Shrine enshrines a local tutelary deity known in legend as Kimata-sama, Ōshige Shrine is identified with “Katsuraki Ōshige Shrine” listed in the Yamato Province, Katsukami District section of the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, making it a Shikinaisha. Its enshrined deity is said to be either Shigeno no Sadamochi or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida.. === Enshrined deities === * Kimata-no-Kami * Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida === Shrine grounds === Komagata-Ōshige Shrine auxiliary shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Auxiliary shrine Stone frog called “Buji Kaeru” at Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|“Buji Kaeru” (Safe-return frog) Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Second torii gate Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio (1).jpg|First torii gate === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|27|17.3|N|135|42|39|E}}}} |Location = Narahara 1662, Gose City, Nara Prefecture |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 17.3 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 39 |Enshrined deities = Kimata-no-Kami, and Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha, former village shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festivals = July 13, October 16 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Komagata-Ōshige Shrine''' (こまがたおおしげじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Narahara, Gose City, Nara Prefecture. === Overview === In 1907 (Meiji 40), Ōshige Shrine, which had been enshrined in the eastern area known as Taguchi, was merged into Komagata Shrine, forming its present configuration.[https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001414.html Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Gose City[https://kotobank.jp/word/駒形大重神社-3081535 Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Kotobank. While Komagata Shrine enshrines a local tutelary deity known in legend as Kimata-sama, Ōshige Shrine is identified with “Katsuraki Ōshige Shrine” listed in the Yamato Province, Katsukami District section of the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, making it a Shikinaisha. Its enshrined deity is said to be either Shigeno no Sadamochi or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida.. === Enshrined deities === * Kimata-no-Kami * Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida === Shrine grounds === Komagata-Ōshige Shrine auxiliary shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Auxiliary shrine Stone frog called “Buji Kaeru” at Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|“Buji Kaeru” (Safe-return frog) Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Second torii gate Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio (1).jpg|First torii gate === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * カツラキ- == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市朝町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:駒形大重神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134928901}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Komagata-Ōshige Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|27|17.3|N|135|42|39|E}}}} |Location = Narahara 1662, Gose City, Nara Prefecture |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 17.3 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 39 |Enshrined deities = Kimata-no-Kami, and Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha, former village shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festivals = July 13, October 16 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Komagata-Ōshige Shrine''' (こまがたおおしげじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Narahara, Gose City, Nara Prefecture. === Overview === In 1907 (Meiji 40), Ōshige Shrine, which had been enshrined in the eastern area known as Taguchi, was merged into Komagata Shrine, forming its present configuration.[https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001414.html Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Gose City[https://kotobank.jp/word/駒形大重神社-3081535 Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Kotobank. While Komagata Shrine enshrines a local tutelary deity known in legend as Kimata-sama, Ōshige Shrine is identified with “Katsuraki Ōshige Shrine” listed in the Yamato Province, Katsukami District section of the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, making it a Shikinaisha. Its enshrined deity is said to be either Shigeno no Sadamochi or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida.. === Enshrined deities === * Kimata-no-Kami * Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida === Shrine grounds === Komagata-Ōshige Shrine auxiliary shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Auxiliary shrine Stone frog called “Buji Kaeru” at Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|“Buji Kaeru” (Safe-return frog) Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Second torii gate Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio (1).jpg|First torii gate === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * カツラキ- == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市朝町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:駒形大重神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134928901}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Komagata-Ōshige Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|27|17.3|N|135|42|39|E}}}} |Location = Narahara 1662, Gose City, Nara Prefecture |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 17.3 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 39 |Enshrined deities = Kimata-no-Kami, and Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha, former village shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festivals = July 13, October 16 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Komagata-Ōshige Shrine''' (こまがたおおしげじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Narahara, Gose City, Nara Prefecture. === Overview === In 1907 (Meiji 40), Ōshige Shrine, which had been enshrined in the eastern area known as Taguchi, was merged into Komagata Shrine, forming its present configuration.[https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001414.html Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Gose City[https://kotobank.jp/word/駒形大重神社-3081535 Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Kotobank. While Komagata Shrine enshrines a local tutelary deity known in legend as Kimata-sama, Ōshige Shrine is identified with “Katsuraki Ōshige Shrine” listed in the Yamato Province, Katsukami District section of the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, making it a Shikinaisha. Its enshrined deity is said to be either Shigeno no Sadamochi or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida.. === Enshrined deities === * Kimata-no-Kami * Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida === Shrine grounds === Komagata-Ōshige Shrine auxiliary shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Auxiliary shrine Stone frog called “Buji Kaeru” at Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|“Buji Kaeru” (Safe-return frog) Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Second torii gate Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio (1).jpg|First torii gate === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * カツラキ- == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '奈良県御所市朝町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:駒形大重神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134928901}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Komagata-Ōshige Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|27|17.3|N|135|42|39|E}}}} |Location = Narahara 1662, Gose City, Nara Prefecture |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 17.3 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 39 |Enshrined deities = Kimata-no-Kami, and Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha, former village shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festivals = July 13, October 16 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Komagata-Ōshige Shrine''' (こまがたおおしげじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Narahara, Gose City, Nara Prefecture. === Overview === In 1907 (Meiji 40), Ōshige Shrine, which had been enshrined in the eastern area known as Taguchi, was merged into Komagata Shrine, forming its present configuration.[https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001414.html Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Gose City[https://kotobank.jp/word/駒形大重神社-3081535 Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Kotobank. While Komagata Shrine enshrines a local tutelary deity known in legend as Kimata-sama, Ōshige Shrine is identified with “Katsuraki Ōshige Shrine” listed in the Yamato Province, Katsukami District section of the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, making it a Shikinaisha. Its enshrined deity is said to be either Shigeno no Sadamochi or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida.. === Enshrined deities === * Kimata-no-Kami * Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida === Shrine grounds === Komagata-Ōshige Shrine auxiliary shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Auxiliary shrine Stone frog called “Buji Kaeru” at Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|“Buji Kaeru” (Safe-return frog) Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Second torii gate Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio (1).jpg|First torii gate === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]]

|Name = Komagata-Ōshige Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|27|17.3|N|135|42|39|E}}}} |Location = Narahara 1662, Gose City, Nara Prefecture |ISO = JP-23 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 27|Latitude seconds = 17.3 |Longitude degrees = 135|Longitude minutes = 42|Longitude seconds = 39 |Enshrined deities = Kimata-no-Kami, and Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = Shikinaisha, former village shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festivals = July 13, October 16 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Nara }} '''Komagata-Ōshige Shrine''' (こまがたおおしげじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Narahara, Gose City, Nara Prefecture. === Overview === In 1907 (Meiji 40), Ōshige Shrine, which had been enshrined in the eastern area known as Taguchi, was merged into Komagata Shrine, forming its present configuration.[https://www.city.gose.nara.jp/kankou/0000001414.html Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Gose City[https://kotobank.jp/word/駒形大重神社-3081535 Komagata-Ōshige Shrine] – Kotobank. While Komagata Shrine enshrines a local tutelary deity known in legend as Kimata-sama, Ōshige Shrine is identified with “Katsuraki Ōshige Shrine” listed in the Yamato Province, Katsukami District section of the *Engishiki Jinmyōchō*, making it a Shikinaisha. Its enshrined deity is said to be either Shigeno no Sadamochi or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida.. === Enshrined deities === * Kimata-no-Kami * Shigeno no Sadamochi, or Katsuragi no Wakainukai Amida === Shrine grounds === Komagata-Ōshige Shrine auxiliary shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Auxiliary shrine Stone frog called “Buji Kaeru” at Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|“Buji Kaeru” (Safe-return frog) Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio.jpg|Second torii gate Komagata-Ōshige Shrine, Narahara, Gose City 2013.5.24 - panoramio (1).jpg|First torii gate === Notes === {{Reflist}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Komagata-Ōshige Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:御所市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gose, Nara]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]]
